name: Izakaya Nonbei
tagline: Intimate Tokyo-style izakaya tucked on a side street — the bartender knows your name by the second visit.
mustOrder: yakitori — grilled over charcoal, proper izakaya style
parking: Street parking on Olu St and Kapahulu side streets
talkStory: If Tokkuri Tei is the boisterous, social izakaya, Nonbei is its quieter, more refined counterpart. Small counter-bar setting that genuinely feels like a side-street izakaya in Tokyo. The chef/owner is Japanese and the food reflects that heritage without compromise. The crowd is local Japanese-American regulars and expats. Tucked on Olu Street off Kapahulu — you have to know it's there. Dinner only, 5pm–late. Reservations recommended.
